content:
Key Findings

  • Rossendale Borough Council processed a total of £67.3M in spending over five financial years.
  • This significant expenditure was spread across 42,536 individual financial transactions.
  • A substantial 100.0% of the council's analysed spending, amounting to £67.3M, is categorised under "Unknown" payment types.
  • The entire dataset for Rossendale Borough Council, covering 2021/22 to 2025/26, lacks specific detail on how payments were made.

Understanding Rossendale's Spending Data

AI DOGE has analysed Rossendale Borough Council's published spending records, covering the financial years from 2021/22 through to 2025/26. This comprehensive review encompasses a total expenditure of £67.3M. This substantial sum is distributed across 42,536 individual transactions, providing a broad overview of the council's financial activities over this five-year period. The data is drawn directly from information published under the Local Government Transparency Code, designed to make public spending more accessible and understandable to citizens.

The purpose of analysing payment types is to offer a deeper insight into how public funds are disbursed. Understanding whether payments are made via BACS, credit card, cheque, or other methods can provide valuable context for financial management and efficiency. It helps citizens and watchdog organisations like AI DOGE to scrutinise spending patterns, identify potential areas for improvement, and ensure accountability in the use of public money. Detailed payment information is a key component of robust financial transparency.

The Challenge of "Unknown" Payment Types

A critical finding from the analysis of Rossendale Borough Council's spending data is the complete absence of categorised payment types. The entire £67.3M of expenditure, representing 100.0% of all analysed transactions, is listed under the "Unknown" payment type. This means that for every single one of the 42,536 transactions, the specific method used to make the payment is not disclosed within the published data. This lack of detail presents a significant challenge for public scrutiny and understanding of the council's financial operations.

When payment types are not specified, it becomes difficult to assess the efficiency or appropriateness of payment methods. For example, some payment methods might incur higher administrative costs or offer less security than others. Without this information, it is impossible for the public to determine if the council is utilising the most cost-effective or secure payment channels for its £67.3M in spending. The "Unknown" classification obscures a fundamental aspect of financial management, hindering comprehensive transparency.

Implications for Public Scrutiny

The categorisation of 100.0% of Rossendale Borough Council's spending as "Unknown" for payment types has direct implications for public scrutiny. Citizens are unable to gain a full picture of how their local council manages its finances. Transparency in local government spending is not just about knowing how much is spent and with whom, but also about understanding the operational details of that spending. The absence of this detail for £67.3M worth of transactions means a key layer of financial information is missing from public view.

For each of the 42,536 transactions analysed, the public cannot ascertain whether the council is using modern, efficient electronic transfers or potentially less efficient methods like cheques. This information gap can lead to questions about the completeness of the data provided under the Local Government Transparency Code. Effective transparency relies on clear, comprehensive, and granular data that allows for meaningful analysis and public engagement with council finances. The current data for Rossendale Borough Council, regarding payment types, falls short of this ideal.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Rossendale Borough Council about the categorisation of payment types for the £67.3M in spending, asking why this information is not published.
  • Attend council committee meetings (they are open to the public) to hear discussions on financial reporting and transparency.
  • Check the council's published contracts register for details on how suppliers are paid, which might offer some additional context.
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of detailed payment type information for public transparency and accountability.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data yourself and compare Rossendale Borough Council's transparency with other Lancashire councils.
This article is based on Rossendale Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
